_meta.data_strategy: Spine pre-enrichment was unusually rich (founded_year, license_holder_name, family_ownership notes, service_mix, leadership names already captured per business). This enrichment layers in: (1) Texas Comptroller entity status — marked 'unknown_pending_lookup' where live fetch could not be performed; (2) Owner age estimates triangulated from founded_year + license tenure proxy + 2nd-gen succession patterns; (3) Coasting tells inferred from website tech profile, no online portal/quote, phone-only contact, no ServiceTitan/FieldEdge/BuildingsOS mentions, aging fleet (truck count vs founding year), owner-as-field-tech patterns; (4) BACnet/EMS capability flagged where website mentions building automation, controls, BMS, or EMS; (5) NEBB/TAB cert flagged where TAB or testing/balancing is on the services page; (6) Successor indicators flagged from spine notes (2nd-gen present, kids in business, transition events).
caveats: Texas Comptroller entity_status not live-fetched in this batch — marked 'unknown_pending_lookup' and flagged for follow-up. None of these 47 businesses are on the PE acquisition exclusion list (already screened at spine step).

Owner age estimates use one of three methods: (a) founder + founding year + assumed age-at-founding 28-32 (high-confidence when 'founded by X in YYYY' on About page); (b) 2nd-gen tenure proxy (owner has been in role N years + assumed age-at-handoff 35-40); (c) trade tenure (license_tenure_proxy when 'N years experience' is the only datapoint). Source tagged per business.
Truck counts are spine-derived ESTIMATES — confirm via Google Maps/site recon before high-conviction outreach.

BACnet/EMS capability is INFERRED from website language (controls, automation, BMS, EMS, Niagara, Distech) — not direct integration verification. Set to true when website mentions building automation/controls capability as core service.
NEBB/TAB certification is INFERRED from services language ('test and balance', 'TAB', 'NEBB certified') — not direct cert directory verification.
ASHRAE membership defaulted to spine value (false unless company explicitly references it on website).

Several businesses in this batch are LARGER than the 10-50 employee ideal (Way Companies, Lochridge-Priest, Young & Pratt, Berger Engineering, A.J. Monier, Gillette, The Porter Co, Kahn Mechanical, Comfort-Air Eng, Kilgore Industries). They remain independent TX-headquartered family/employee-owned — flag for scoring not exclusion.

is_distressed defaulted to false unless spine independence_verified=false or independence_notes flagged anomalies (e.g., Houk Air SE Acquisition Sub flag, LEX Air affiliation concern). These get is_distressed=false but flagged in coasting_tells/notes for follow-up.

dba_name: Way Service, Ltd. / The Way Companies
owner_name: Way family (4th generation; specific principal name not disclosed on About page)
owner_age_source: fourth_generation_succession_proxy (4-gen succession over 108 yrs → current principal likely 55-65)
coasting_tells: 108-year operator — extreme tenure with low public-facing owner identity
About page does not name current principal — opacity is a tell
Phone-only contact + form (no online customer portal/quote)
Multi-location TX+LA but no obvious cloud-native ops tech
notes: Larger than 10-50 ideal but explicit family ownership over 4 generations. Flag for scoring; outreach to Way family principal.

owner_name: Charlotte Kahn (President), Josh Kahn (Visionary)
owner_age_source: founder_succession_proxy (Kahn founded 1974; Charlotte as President + Josh as Visionary suggests 2nd-gen succession; ~62 for Charlotte)
coasting_tells: WBE-certified since 1995 — Charlotte Kahn as owner means she's been principal for 30+ years
No online quote/customer portal observed on website
Leadership page lists Charlotte + Josh but no public successor plan
notes: Charlotte Kahn likely 60-65; WBE-certified means woman-owned status reportable. Josh Kahn (Visionary role) signals 2nd-gen has been integrated. Mid-range succession candidate.

owner_name: David Akins (VP); principal owners not disclosed publicly
owner_age_source: founding_tenure_proxy (1963 founding + 63-yr operation + locally-owned messaging → senior principal ~60-70)
coasting_tells: 63-yr operator — owner age very high probability >55
Multi-location (Waco, Temple, Corsicana) but single principal disclosed
notes: Largest Central TX mechanical company, ~$83M revenue. Larger than 10-50 ideal — flag for scoring.

owner_name: David Zern (Owner/President; son of Joseph Zern who was co-owner from 1967)
owner_age_source: second_generation_succession_proxy (Joseph co-owner from 1967; David Zern as son and current Owner/President suggests assumed control 1990s → 62 now)
coasting_tells: 104-year operator — owner age 60+ very high probability
Single principal disclosed (David Zern) — succession plan not public
85+ team but no online customer portal/quote on website
notes: Strong candidate: David Zern is 2nd-gen owner of 104-yr business. Prime age window for sale conversation.

legal_name: The Porter Company / Porter Service Co.
owner_name: Aaron Richards (President); David Sietsema (CFO)
owner_age_source: role_tenure_proxy (Aaron Richards as current President of 81-yr company suggests mid-career executive ~50-60)
coasting_tells: 81-year operator with semiconductor fab + pharma clients (commercial+industrial)
President + CFO disclosed but Porter family not visible — possibly non-family successor regime
Specialty piping/clean room suggests technical depth but ops modernization unclear
notes: Family-owned per spine but no Porter family member visible — possibly already transitioned to professional management; flag for entity check.

owner_name: 100+ employee-owners (ESOP structure)
owner_age_source: esop_no_single_owner
coasting_tells: Re-org 1972 by Richard L. Berger — Berger family no longer single principal
notes: ESOP structure means succession-by-sale unlikely — score down. Independent and TX-based but not single-owner.

owner_name: Gillette family (3rd generation; founded by Vincent Gillette Sr. and Marjorie Gillette in 1959)
owner_age_source: third_generation_succession_proxy (Vincent Sr. + Marjorie founded 1959; 3rd-gen now active → ~50-60)
coasting_tells: 67-year operator — well established, prime succession window
250 employees but 3rd-gen family-owned — succession-or-sale conversation real
notes: Larger than 10-50 ideal but 3-gen family ownership + 67 yrs. Strong succession candidate; flag for scoring.

owner_name: Jeff Holt (2nd gen; brother Mark Holt passed 2019; father Dan Holt founded 1972)

coasting_tells: 54-year operator, 2nd-gen owner ~62, brother passed 2019 (sibling co-owner now gone)
Brother's death is a classic succession trigger event
60 employees, no online customer portal observed
TACLA3476C license (early TACLA number = long tenure)
notes: PRIME succession candidate. 2nd-gen owner ~62, brother+co-owner died 2019, 60-employee mid-size shop. Commercial-only focus. Highly investigable.

owner_name: Jeffrey Beyer (President)

notes: 3 Beyer brothers across 3 separate companies — interesting structure. Family-owned, owner age ~60. Sibling group dynamics could create sale event.
